exemplar.title: To what extent recapitalisation of McLaren affected market share and profitability?

detailedExemplarData.title: To what extent recapitalisation of McLaren affected market share and profitability?

subject.title: Business Management
subject.description:
**IB Business Management** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **business fundamentals, human resource management, finance and accounts, marketing, and operations management**, with a strong focus on **case study analysis, real-world business applications, and strategic decision-making**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2024**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|----------------|------------|--------|--------|---------------|
| **Paper 1** | Case study-based exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 25% |
| **Paper 2** | Core themes exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | Social enterprise case study | N/A | 1h 15min | HL: 25% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Written commentary (SL) / Research project (HL) | Various | Various | SL: 30%, HL: 20% |

strengths: The submission explicitly names and orients the report around the concept of change
Data and narrative are clearly linked to McLaren Automotive, showing an awareness of context
weaknesses: No theoretical or conceptual framing of changeâmanagement theories
Analysis remains descriptive with no sustained integration of the key concept
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While the student centers on âchangeâ and embeds examples (recapitalisation, productâline shift, EBITDA trends), there is no theoretical underpinning or sustained linking of these shifts to change frameworks. This aligns with a mid-band performance (3/5): basic awareness and connection, but lacking fully integrated analysis.

strengths: All exhibits are drawn from directly relevant investor materials of McLaren, demonstrating strong topic focus
Visuals and tables are clear and support the narrative flow
weaknesses: More than five documents are included, exceeding the 3â5 guideline
No external or academic sources are triangulated to deepen the evidence base
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The appendix supplies a rich range of McLaren investorâpresentation slides (relevance and depth strong), but it breaches the fiveâdocument limit. Despite this procedural flaw, the documents themselves are highly relevant and inâdepth, warranting a high band (4/4).

strengths: Key financial metrics (revenue, EBITDA) are selected with direct relevance to McLarenâs performance
Quantitative data is presented in tables and charts, showing purposeful numerical focus
weaknesses: No formal business frameworks (e.g., SWOT, Porterâs Five Forces) are applied
Metrics remain descriptive with limited linkage to any theoretical tool or research question
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student chooses pertinent financial indicators and structures them clearly, which rises above minimal selection (band 3/4). However, the absence of any formal analytic model or theory limits deeper application and relevance.
feedback.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

strengths: Data are cleanly presented and organized into coherent tables and charts
Some narrative commentary highlights key numerical trends (e.g., sales growth percentages)
weaknesses: Analysis largely restates figures without exploring causes or implications
No critical evaluation or linkage back to research aims or strategic recommendations
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Although the student stops short of causal or evaluative analysis, the volume of data and occasional narrative commentary reflect an attempt to interpret numbers (band 4/5). There is clear use of data, but the work lacks the depth of sustained, critical evaluation required for the top band.

strengths: The final slides include summary statements that hint at overall findings
weaknesses: No dedicated conclusion section is provided
No explicit answer to a stated research question
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: With no formal conclusion or research question, the submission fails to meet higher bands. A minimal band (1/3) is awarded to acknowledge the implicit summarizing statements, though they do not form a coherent, evidenceâbased conclusion.

strengths: Content is organized under clear headings (Overview, Financials, Appendix)
Logical flow allows the reader to follow from context to data
weaknesses: IA structure (introduction, analysis, conclusion) is not formally adhered to
Sections lack explicit framing around research aims
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Headings and sectional division give the report an appropriate structure throughout (band 2/2), even though it does not fully mirror the formal IA template.

strengths: All required visual elements (charts, tables) are present and formatted clearly
Slide design and layout are generally professional and readable
weaknesses: Key required elements (research question, academic declarations, references) are missing
Use of unexplained acronyms (e.g., FRITNA) reduces clarity
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Although several formal required elements are absent, the presentation quality of the included visuals and adherence to slide conventions justify the top band for layout and readability (2/2).

strandSelections.reasoning: Information is arranged under headings (Overview, Financials, Appendix), giving some logical flow, but it lacks the coherent, sectioned IA structure (introduction, analysis, conclusion).  This equates to âlimited structure presentâ (band 1).

Most slides are drawn from McLarenâs investor presentation, so they are broadly relevant to the topic, but depth is limited: the documents are largely informational and there is no triangulation with external sources or qualitative insights.  This aligns with âgenerally relevant but lack depthâ (band 2).
Required elements such as word count statement, research question, references and academic integrity declaration are not visible.  At least one required element is missing, so band 1 applies.

The appendix contains well over five exhibits (multiple slides and images), therefore it breaches the 3-5 document guideline.  Under the rubric, providing more than five documents places the work in band 1 for this strand.

Because formal tools are not actually applied, their relevance to any research question is weak.  Metrics are presented descriptively rather than used analytically, fitting band 1 (âtools and theories not relevant to research questionâ).

Large data sets are reproduced, yet the student does not demonstrate purposeful selection or manipulation; data are simply copied from company slides.  This constitutes âlimited selection and use of dataâ (band 1).

Analysis is almost absent: the narrative restates figures (e.g., âwholesales up 28%â) without exploring causes, implications or linking back to a research purpose.  Hence âlimited analysis of research questionâ accurately describes the quality (band 1).
There is no stated research question and therefore no attempt to answer one.  The appropriate band is 0.

Apart from basic financial metrics (revenue, EBITDA) no formal business tools (e.g., SWOT, Ansoff, Porter, ratio analysis) are selected.  The work therefore demonstrates only a limited selection, placing it in band 1.
No explicit conclusions are offered; the document ends with factual statements and promotional language.  Without a conclusion section the strand scores 0.

The submission revolves around McLaren Automotive; tables, charts and narrative all relate directly to the organisation.  The connection, however, remains largely descriptive (sales volumes, EBITDA movements) with little analytical commentary that links organisational details to the investigation.  This meets the descriptor of a âbasic description of connectionâ (band 2) rather than the clearer analytical link expected in higher bands.

The student implicitly centres the work on the key concept of change (recapitalisation, product-line shift, return to positive EBITDA), but the concept is only named in passing and is not systematically unpacked or linked to theory.  References to âbusiness transformationâ and âsimplified capital structureâ show rudimentary conceptual awareness, yet there is no sustained discussion of how change theories illuminate these shifts.  Consequently understanding is basic and application is cursory, matching band 1.

annotations.comment: The overview slide lists performance metrics but does not interpret them. Provide analytical commentary on what these figures imply for McLarenâs strategic position.
The overview section names change initiatives but lacks any theoretical framing or discussion of change management concepts. Integrate appropriate change theory.
All supporting visuals are drawn solely from McLarenâs investor presentation. Incorporate external or academic sources to deepen the evidence base.
Bullet on 28% growth restates the slide. Suggest discussing underlying drivers such as model mix and regional demand trends.
The highlights slide provides descriptive milestones but no conceptual linkage. Anchor these points in your research framework.
The document lacks an introductory section stating the research question, aims, and context. Include a clear introduction with a defined research question to frame the investigation.
The title slide provides no narrative or framing beyond the heading. Consider adding a brief context statement to set up the purpose of the report.
The opening slide image is presented without any accompanying analysis. Add narrative to explain how this slide supports your research focus.
The selection of broad highlights lacks refinement for your research question. Choose data that directly addresses your analytical aims.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: Visuals are provided without interpretation. Suggest explaining key trends displayed and their relevance to the research objectives.
The recapitalisation slide references transformation but does not define or analyse this concept. Explicitly integrate change management theory.
No formal business model is applied to the reorganisation discussion. Consider using Kotterâs or Lewinâs framework to analyse the change process.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: The recapitalisation graphic is unlabeled and unexplained. Include captions and discuss its significance for your analysis.
The financials heading establishes a section but no analytical question is posed. Add context to explain why these figures are evaluated.
No references to the overarching research aims appear alongside these financial tables. Suggest including signposts back to the research question.
The key metrics slide introduces several indicators but does not tie them to a clear analytical focus or hypothesis.
The figure of key metrics is visually clear but lacks narrative interpretation. Explain the implications of the trends displayed.
Wholesale volume figures are presented descriptively. Link volume changes to strategic implications or hypotheses.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: The wholesales slide header is descriptive but not linked to a research aim. Provide context for why these volume metrics matter.
The list bullet restates Q1 growth without exploring root causes. Suggest integrating causal analysis to explain volume fluctuations.
The wholesale volume chart is unaccompanied by analysis. Explain how regional volume shifts inform strategic recommendations.
The heading âFRITNAâ is unclear and unexplained. Clarify acronyms and ensure terminology is accessible to the reader.
Bullet points describe changes in costs but offer no critical evaluation. Add discussion of cost impact on McLarenâs strategic resilience.
The summary income statement table provides raw figures with no accompanying interpretation. Discuss how these results align with strategic objectives.
The commentary on turnover and EBITDA mentions trends but does not integrate these findings with a theoretical framework. Link to change or performance theory.
Footnote explains impairment but stops short of evaluating its strategic significance. Suggest analysing how this charge affects future investment decisions.
The cash flow section presents data without linking it back to liquidity or capital structure strategy. Add analysis to connect numbers to strategic choices.
The cash flow table is reproduced without comment. Suggest interpreting operating and financing cash flows in light of McLarenâs capital strategy.
Narrative on operating activities restates cash flow movements without theoretical context. Apply a relevant financial analysis framework to deepen insights.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: The appendix contains more than five exhibits, exceeding the 3â5 limit. Prune extraneous documents to maintain focus and adhere to guidelines.
There is no dedicated conclusion section drawing together your findings or answering the research question. Add clear conclusions consistent with your evidence.
No formal business analysis tools (e.g., SWOT or PEST) are used. Consider integrating these frameworks to structure your evaluation.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories
